articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Landlord Concerns:** A staggering 82% of landlords are worried about the implications of the Renters’ Rights Act, with 44% expressing significant anxiety.  
- **Portfolio Shrinking:** Three-quarters of landlords feel that new regulations deter them from expanding their portfolios, with half considering divesting or reducing their investments within the next two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords urged to boost EPC ratings to attract tenants ahead of the 2030 mandate for a minimum C rating.  
- Rising energy costs due to the Iran War make energy efficiency a top priority for tenants seeking savings.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Only 25% of UK landlords believe that new planning reforms will benefit their operations.  
- Confidence in planning changes remains low among property investors, underscoring concerns about the effectiveness of proposed reforms.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Hidden Leak Alert:** Over half of hidden leaks in properties stem from external sources, underscoring the need for proactive outdoor pipe maintenance for landlords.  
- **Costly Consequences:** Failure to address these leaks could lead to significant repair costs, potentially running into thousands of pounds.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- Major changes under the Renters’ Rights Act will transform the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales, effective 1 May 2026, focusing on balancing landlord and tenant rights.  
- Landlords are urged to prepare by following top tips to navigate the new requirements and regulatory oversight set to take effect soon.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Shockingly Unprepared:** 84% of landlords lack readiness for the upcoming Renters’ Rights Act set to launch on May 1.  
- **Compliance Crisis:** 58% of landlords face a significant risk of non-compliance, raising major concerns ahead of the implementation.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting strategies from expansion to restructuring portfolios in response to slowing rental growth, as indicated by the latest ONS data.  
- The focus is now on optimizing existing assets and financing rather than solely increasing supply to meet demand.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals that landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- Increased competition from these groups is making it increasingly difficult for new entrants to secure homes.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the upcoming government to acknowledge the vital role of the private rented sector (PRS) with a comprehensive strategy.  
- There’s a growing concern that without formal recognition, the sector may face challenges that could drive landlords away.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting May, new HMRC regulations mandate tax advisor registration for conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams, risking penalties of up to £10,000 for non-compliance.
- Landlords need to stay vigilant about these changes to avoid hefty fines associated with the new tax advisor registration requirements.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A UK council asserts that implementing licensing fees won't result in increased rents for tenants.  
- Landlords face a £694 fee per property, which the council does not anticipate will be recouped.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook announces that the party will not implement rent controls in the UK housing market.  
- The decision highlights Labour's focus on other strategies to address housing affordability without imposing rent restrictions.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council aims to regulate the rising trend of unregulated Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) to ensure safer living conditions.  
- New detailed guidelines for HMO landlords are in development to curb the current "free for all" situation.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign kicks off on Monday to educate tenants on their rights under the Renters Rights Act.  
- The initiative aims to empower renters by highlighting their legal protections and options in the housing market.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act has led to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, aligning with expert predictions.  
- Property expert Paul Shamplina anticipates ongoing challenges for renters as a result of these legislative changes.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are increasing as the implementation of the Renters Rights Act approaches, signaling potential changes in the rental market.  
- The latest data released by lettings agency Hamptons highlights the ongoing upward trend in rental prices.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act, set to be enacted in just 10 days, is poised to eliminate hobbyist landlords from the rental market.
- Critics argue that the legislation will significantly impact small-scale landlords, potentially leading to a reduction in available rental properties.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Gen Z Entrepreneurs Embrace Property Investment**: Despite facing increased regulations and taxes, young investors are actively entering the property market to grow their wealth.  
- **Overcoming Challenges**: These budding entrepreneurs are navigating the complexities of property investment, showcasing a determination to succeed in a challenging economic landscape.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- House buyers are facing a challenging market, leading many sellers to reduce asking prices, experience failed sales, or withdraw listings altogether, according to House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson.  
- The current conditions signal a tough climate for home sellers, impacting their ability to move properties efficiently.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is intensifying oversight on property valuations for Inheritance Tax, resulting in a surge of cases sent to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- This increased scrutiny is contributing to a rise in Inheritance Tax revenues for the UK government.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A property listing for a £135k terrace home in Penrith, Cumbria, inadvertently features an asleep tenant in its promotional photos and video.  
- The unexpected appearance has sparked attention and amusement, highlighting an unusual oversight in real estate marketing.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Referral fees are described as an "unseen surcharge" by the Conveyancing Task Force, effectively labeling them a hidden tax on homebuyers.
- This revelation highlights the financial burden placed on buyers in the UK property market, raising concerns over transparency and fair practices.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond is expanding into Scotland, with Clyde Property Group managing its West of Scotland portfolio.  
- David Alexander emphasizes the growth strategy as part of Lomond's northern expansion.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue, attributing the decline to unexpected geopolitical challenges.  
- CEO Guy Gittins highlights that new buyer activity has significantly fallen short of expectations.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Rising inflation in the UK is primarily fueled by escalating prices in fuel and transport due to the Middle East conflict.
- Experts warn that these price increases are likely to ripple throughout the broader economy soon.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Market Access Crisis:** A staggering nine out of ten new homes built in the UK are never offered on the open sales market, highlighting a significant gap in housing availability.  
- **Expert Insight:** Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto, sheds light on this concerning trend, emphasizing the implications for buyers and the property industry.
